name: Chili with Beans
recipeIngredient: 4 pounds Lean beef sirloin, chopped into 1/2 inch cubes
3 tablespoons New Mexico (or other hot) Chili powder
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash beans in a colander and soak overnight.
2. The next day, wash the beans again and place them in a large chili pot.
3. Cover the beans with water, about 1/2 to 3/4 inch over the beans, and simmer, covered, for about 2-1/2 hours or until done.
4. If substituting beans with lean beef sirloin, brown the meat (1 pound at a time) in a non-stick skillet without any oil.
5. Place the browned meat in the large chili pot.
6. Add an additional 12 ounce can of tomato sauce and 1 to 2 cans of fat-skimmed beef stock to the meat mixture.
7. Add all other ingredients (onions, garlic powder, red cayenne pepper, New Mexico Chili powder, dried oregano, ground cumin, vegetable oil, and Louisiana Hot Sauce) to a large saucepan.
8. Mix well in the saucepan.
9. Simmer the saucepan mixture for 1 hour.
10. When the beans are done, add the contents of the saucepan to the chili pot and simmer for an additional 30 to 45 minutes.
11. Makes 15 to 20 servings, with each serving containing approximately 225 calories (205 calories without oil).
12. Salt and pepper to taste.
